## PAGE 00001: 2024x Refresh1 Version News

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### UAF 1.2 Plugin

Released on: July 5, 2024

Beginning with version 2024x Refresh1, significant enhancements have been made to various diagramming tools and tables. The **Security Connectivity** and **Security Role-based Connectivity** tables have been restructured into dedicated diagrams for better clarity on Operational and Resource viewpoints. The **Strategic Driver Map** now visually connects Drivers, Enterprise Goals, and Strategic Phases, while the new **Strategic Drivers Table** organizes elements, such as Drivers, Challenges, Opportunities, and Capabilities, in a structured format. The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog has been introduced for easier management and preview of affected diagrams, with new options for updating diagram elements. Enhancements to **Gantt Charts** include better project and milestone management and display options for projects. Measurement management has been improved with features for reusing default values and auto-populating measurements in Property Sets. Additionally, model conversion between frameworks and new validation rules have been implemented to streamline processes and ensure data integrity.

To download the latest version, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files).For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2).

### Diagraming

##### Security Connectivity and Role-Based Connectivity Tables

Beginning with version 2024x Refresh1, the Security Connectivity and Security Role-based Connectivity tables have been redesigned into dedicated diagrams focusing on the Operational and Resource viewpoints:

- Security Connectivity (Resource) Table
- Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table
- Security Connectivity (Operational) Table
- Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Strategic Driver Map

This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Strategic Driver Table

The new Strategic Drivers Table allows you to assemble Drivers, Challenges, Opportunities, and Capabilities in a tabular form in strict order: the Driver presenting Challenges, the Challenges motivating Opportunities, and the Opportunities impacting Capabilities.

In this table, you can also define relationships between Drivers and Challenges, Challenges and Opportunities, Opportunities and Capabilities. This table captures the Driver, Challenge, Opportunity, and Capability elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected Diagrams Preview Dialog

To enhance the exchange creation and management experience in Resource, Service, and Operational viewpoints, we have introduced the **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog. This dialog conveniently presents all affected diagrams, allowing you to preview them individually. Furthermore, the affected elements, where the created Exchanges will be realized, are prominently marked in the diagram preview pane.

During the preview process, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for diagrams in which you wish to display the Conveyed Items of the selected exchange.

The new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams**controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Report templates

- The new report templates have been implemented for Service Connectivity Table and Service Role-based Connectivity Table. The reports are available through the Report Wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A new Strategic Driver Table also makes a new report available. You can create the report through the Report Wizard, directly from the table toolbar, or using a quick report option from the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Gantt Chart Enhancements

##### Project Roadmap Enhancements

- The new Actual Project Assignment wizard will help you add new Actual Projects to the chart more conveniently. With this wizard, you will:
  - Have the ability to reuse existing Projects as created Actual Project types [ATTACHMENT filename='actual_project_assignmnet_wizard.png']
  - Control Actual Projects hierarchy creation based on the existing Project structure.
- Two additional steps were added to the Actual Project Milestone Creation wizard. With these steps, you can specify the Actual Project Milestone Version Released and Version Withdrawn properties during milestone creation. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- The new**Display Actual Projects as Grouping Elements**option (located in the Gantt chart options area) is introduced. This option controls how the Actual Project is displayed: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']>>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Turn the option on to display the Actual Project only as a grouping element.
  - Turn the option off to display the grouping Actual Project bars with Actual Project Milestones and completion progress.

##### Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Enhancements

- You can now choose whether you want to show or hide selected Capability's Provisions in the Capability Provisions area in the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart. [ATTACHMENT filename='show_hide_capability_provisions_area_strategic_phasing.png']
- Now, when creating a Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, you can choose to add Capabilities and Resources from the used projects. These elements will be displayed in gray to indicate that they come from a used project.

##### Other

- The Gantt charts were enhanced to indicate when an element is used from another project and/or is read-only, with the element name displayed in gray to signify this status.

### Exchange Management

- The functionality when reconnecting connectors between elements was improved, offering options for managing exchanges. Now, the modeling tool checks the flow properties of source and target ports (if defined), recommending suitable exchanges for realization. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']>>]]>
- You can now specify a Conveyed Item directly in the diagram, picking the needed Conveyed Item element from the diagram palette and dragging it on to the exchange. [ATTACHMENT filename='set_conveyed_item_palette_personnel.png']
- Now the Conveyed Items in the Realized Exchange wizards are represented with icons to help you identify the Conveyed Items.
- With this version, when you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements as well. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing**[Project option](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Setting+project+options)controls the arrow visibility.
- You can now easily navigate from the selected exchange in Containment tree to the diagrams where that exchange is realized. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Measurement Management Enhancements

With this version, we have enhanced measurements management for Property Sets and Measurable Elements.

- Before the 2024xR1 release, you had the ability to define actual values for all measurements within Measurement Sets or Property Sets. Now, with the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you can effortlessly reuse default actual values to define the entire structure of Actual Property Sets. This enhancement includes the implementation of the Default value button, conveniently accessible within the Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Actual Measurements' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Prior to the 2024xR1 release, automatic population of measurements was limited to Measurable Elements only, with no capability for Property Sets. With the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you now have the ability to automatically populate measurements on Property Sets as well. Measurements are created for elements with the **Applies for** tag values (stereotyped with «DoDAFProperties») applied, and the newly created measurements are typed by the elements with the «DoDAFProperties» stereotype applied. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Before the 2024x R1 release, reusing and synchronization of Measurement structures was not feasible in UAF. With the release of the 2024x R1, you can now leverage this functionality for Property Set usages, including Assets and Roles, as well as Measurement Sets and Measurements. This enhancement enables the creation of an Actual Property Set structure suggested based on the structure of the Property Set typing the Measurable Element, with default values also suggested.

Existing Actual Property Sets of the Measurable Element type are recommended, and this allows you to review and adjust them as needed. Additionally, the newly created Actual Property Set is applied as a default value of the Measurable Element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Model Conversion Between Frameworks Improvements

The project conversion between different Enterprise Architecture Frameworks has undergone some improvements and now adds more clarity. The framework packages are seamlessly mapped during conversion, with package names adjusted accordingly. Custom-named packages remain unchanged, while multiple packages with identical names are merged into one.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Validation

- The COR2185 validation rule was improved to synchronize Measurement Sets of the validated element, located in the Measurement Set property, with Measurement Sets of the associated element: extending beyond Measurable Elements to include Property sets that own the measurements typed by Measurement Sets.
- New completeness validation rules (COM2028, COM2029, COM2030) have been implemented for Operational, Resource, and Service viewpoints to highlight exchanges that are not realized by any realizing element.
- The new correctness validation rule COR2184was implemented to check whether the Actual Project Start and End dates enclose the dates of its owned Actual Projects.
- The COR2196 correctness validation rule was implemented to check whether the date of the Actual Project Milestone does not precede the date of its referenced Actual Project Milestones.

### Discontinued Features

- The PES export functionality is no longer available.

### Other Enhancements

- One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Operational/Resource/Resource Service/Service/ Interface, which has the same features as its original Interface except that its Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with conjugated Interfaces' space='']>>]]>
- You can now create a Definition and Comment for any UAF element in their**Specification**window>**Documentation/Comment**property group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- The new Project option **Show Diagram Abbreviations**has beenintroduced. Set this option to true to show diagram abbreviation in the Containment tree. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree' space='']>>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- With the new Project Option **Show default documentation for UAF Packages**, you canchoose to show the default UAF Packagedocumentation. Turning the option on will display the documentationin:
  - The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property
  - The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the Model Browser. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Show default documentation for UAF packages' space='']>>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='default_package_documentation.png']
- The Strategic Motivation Table has been improved. Now when you click the **Add New** or **Add Nested** button, the SysML Requirements are collapsed and you can focus only on UAF requirements - Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective.
- The creation process of UAF Instance Specifications was enhanced by enabling the creation of slots when one instance specification is dragged and dropped onto another from the diagram, diagram palette, or Containment tree when the structure of Instance Specification types is predefined.
- The role-based connectivity diagrams have been enhanced by adding a Context column to show the context of the element realizing the exchange .

Now, with the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you can effortlessly reuse default actual values to define the entire structure of Actual Property Sets. This enhancement includes the implementation of the <strong>Default value</strong> button, conveniently accessible within the Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Prior to the 2024xR1 release, automatic population of measurements was limited to Measurable Elements only, with no capability for Property Sets. With the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you now have the ability to automatically populate measurements on Property Sets as well. Measurements are created for elements with the <strong>Applies for</strong> tag values (stereotyped with «DoDAFProperties») applied, and the newly created measurements are typed by the elements with the «DoDAFProperties» stereotype applied. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_applyFor_tag.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><ul><li>Before the 2024x R1 release, reusing and synchronization of Measurement structures was not feasible in UAF. With the release of the 2024x R1, you can now leverage this functionality for Property Set usages, including Assets and Roles, as well as Measurement Sets and Measurements. This enhancement enables the creation of an Actual Property Set structure suggested based on the structure of the Property Set typing the Measurable Element, with default values also suggested.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Existing Actual Property Sets of the Measurable Element type are recommended, and this allows you to review and adjust them as needed. Additionally, the newly created Actual Property Set is applied as a default value of the Measurable Element.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reusing measurements structure owned by roles type.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Model Conversion Between Frameworks Improvements</h1><p>The project conversion between different Enterprise Architecture Frameworks has undergone some improvements and now adds more clarity. The framework packages are seamlessly mapped during conversion, with package names adjusted accordingly. Custom-named packages remain unchanged, while multiple packages with identical names are merged into one.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_frameworks_wn.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Validation</h1><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The COR2185 validation rule was improved to synchronize Measurement Sets of the validated element, located in the Measurement Set property, with Measurement Sets of the associated element: extending beyond Measurable Elements to include Property sets that own the measurements typed by Measurement Sets. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">New completeness validation rules (<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">COM2028, COM2029, COM2030) have been implemented for Operational, Resource, and Service viewpoints to highlight exchanges that are not realized by any realizing element. </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The new correctness validation rule <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">COR2184 </span>was implemented to check whether the Actual Project Start and End dates enclose the dates of its owned <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Actual Projects. </span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The COR2196 correctness validation rule was implemented to check whether the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7cd7f86-4c77-45ad-beb5-754b7039fccd">date</ac:inline-comment-marker> of the Actual Project Milestone does not precede the date of its referenced Actual Project Milestones. </span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Discontinued Features</h1><ul><li><span>The PES export functionality is no longer available. </span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Other Enhancements</h1></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Operational/Resource/Resource Service/Service/ Interface, which has the same features as its original Interface except that its Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with conjugated Interfaces" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">You can now create a Definition and Comment for any UAF element in their </span><strong>Specification<span> </span></strong>window<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>&gt; </span><strong>Documentation/Comment</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition_creation.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The new Project option <strong style="text-align: left;">Show Diagram Abbreviations </strong>has been<span> introduced. Set this option to true to show diagram abbreviation in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5cf373e3-512c-43a2-88d4-7cf632a318bd">Containment tree</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_abbreviations_sample.png" /></ac:image></span></span></li><li><p><span>With the new Project Option <strong style="text-align: left;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</strong>, y</span>ou <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">can</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">choose to show the default UAF Package</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">documentation. Turning the option on will display the documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">in:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li>The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property</li><li>The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the<span> </span>Model Browser.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_package_documentation.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span>The Strategic Motivation Table has been improved. Now when you click the <strong>Add New</strong> or <strong>Add Nested</strong> button, the SysML Requirements are collapsed and you can focus only on UAF requirements - Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective. </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span>The creation process of UAF Instance Specifications was enhanced by enabling the creation of slots when one instance specification is dragged and dropped onto another from the diagram, diagram palette, or Containment tree <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="00bf4a89-3c01-4f93-b81c-058fe8451b8c">when</ac:inline-comment-marker> the structure of Instance Specification types is predefined.</span></span></li><li>The role-based connectivity diagrams have been enhanced by adding a <span>Context column to show the context of the element realizing the exchange</span>.  </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194643207 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=4 -->
## PAGE 00002: 2024x Refresh2 Version News

- page_id: `194643207`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/194643207/2024x+Refresh2+Version+News
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-11-27T16:32:16.214+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### UAF 1.2 Plugin

Released on: November 8, 2024

The **2024x Refresh2** release introduces key enhancements to Gantt charts, diagramming, and other capabilities, aimed at improving clarity and usability. The **Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart**now includes domain-based color-coding, and a new hierarchical view has been added to the Strategic **Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table**. Other updates include enhanced element creation in UAF maps and a consistent approach to descriptions across matrices and tables.

To download the latest version, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files).For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2).

### Gantt Chart Improvements

- Now, in the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, Capability Provision area, the bars color is shown according to the domain. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_phasing_colors.png']
- In the 2024x Refresh2 version, the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart has been improved. If there are duplicate Exhibit relationships connecting the same element pairs, they will now be displayed as a single bar in the Gantt Chart. [ATTACHMENT filename='Entry aggregation.png']
- You can now adjust the visible part of the Gantt chart bars. You can either shift the bars to the left based on the selected bar's start date and marking options, or keep the default view, determined by the earliest bar date and marking options. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Beginning with this version, you can view and resolve failing validation(s) directly in the Project Roadmap Gantt charts. Actual Project Milestones are now highlighted when at least one validation rule fails. [ATTACHMENT filename='validation_gantt_chart.png']
- The Personnel Availability Gantt Chart has been enhanced. Now, the Actual Persons that were removed from a chart (Remove from Chart action) are no longer displayed, regardless of whether the **Display Actual Persons in Different Lines** option is enabled or disabled.

### Diagramming

- Several improvements have been added to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table' space=''] , including:
  - Implementation of a hierarchical view of instances in both Compact and Complete tree display modes.
  - Updates to the functionality for adding new or existing items.
  - Enhancements to the All View report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Other Enhancements

- UAF requirement refactoring has been enhanced to improve identifier prefix updates. Now, when you refactor UAF requirements, the identifier prefixes are also updated. For example, refactoring an Enterprise Goal to an Enterprise Objective changes the prefix from EG to EO, and vice versa.
- The process for creating elements in the UAF predefined map has been enhanced. When the scope is set in the relation map, the owner of the created elements will now be the same as the diagram owner.
- The owning package selection in Gantt Charts, Forecast Tables, and Strategic Drivers Table wizards has been improved. You can now filter to display only packages or both types and packages to be selected as owners.
- The description area for matrices now displays the information in the same way as t he description areas of the tables and maps. [Lear more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Working+with+matrices#Workingwithmatrices-Descriptionarea)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926427 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00003: 2024x Version News

- page_id: `191926427`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926427/2024x+Version+News
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:50.487+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### UAF 1.2 Plugin

Released on: November 10, 2023

In the 2024x version of UAF, significant enhancements have been introduced. Behavior to Structure Synchronization has been enriched, allowing the automatic construction of structure models from defined activities and enabling one-click generation of Internal Connectivity diagrams.

Diagramming improvements include specialized Security Process Flow diagrams aligned with Operational and Resource viewpoints and new NAFv4 Structure diagrams for Logical, Service, and Physical Resource Specification Viewpoints.

Additionally, the Strategic Viewpoint now offers simplified assembly tools, such as the Strategic Motivation Table and the Impact Matrix, enhancing analysis and traceability. Exchange management has been refined for better visibility control, and various dialogs have been optimized for efficient review and management. These enhancements, along with improved library support and renamed perspectives, provide you with a more intuitive and efficient modeling experience.

To download the latest version, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files).For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2).

- [UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-UAFBehaviortoStructureSynchronization)
- [Diagramming](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-Diagramming)
  - New Security Process Flow diagrams
  - New NAFv4 Structure diagrams
  - New views in Strategic Viewpoint

- [Exchange management improvements](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-Exchangemanagementimprovements)
  - Exchange visibility management
  - Realized Exchange wizard improvements
  - Exchange Manager improvements
  - Producing and Consuming Functions dialog improvements

- [Library Support](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-LibrarySupport)
- [Templates and Perspectives](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-TemplatesandPerspectives)
- [Other Improvements](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-OtherImprovements)

### UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization

In the 2024x version, the UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization has been enhanced. Now, you can automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and generate an Internal Connectivity diagram by executing a single-click command.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating structure models by behavior models' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Diagramming

##### New Security Process Flow diagrams

With version 2024x, the Security Process flow diagram has been specialized to align with two distinct Operational and Resource viewpoints, which led to the creation of two new diagrams: Security Process Flow (Operational) and Security Process Flow (Resource).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### New NAFv4 Structure diagrams

New structure diagrams have been implemented in the Logical, Service, and Physical Resource Specification Viewpoints:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S2 Service Structure' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P2 Resource Structure' space='']

##### New views in Strategic Viewpoint

Beginning with this version, the following new views related to the Strategic Viewpoint are introduced:

- Strategic Motivation Table , which you can find under the UAF Strategy Diagrams category, is designed to simplify the assembly of UAF Strategic requirements, such as Enterprise Goals and Enterprise Objectives, in a tabular format.
- Impact Matrix , which you can find under the UAF Analysis Diagrams category, illustrates the mapping between the Strategic, Operational, Resource, Service Viewpoint Elements and Strategic Viewpoint Elements using the Impacted By relationship.

### Exchange management improvements

This version adds significant enhancements to working with exchanges, including the ability to control their visibility on diagrams. Improved exchange creation wizards and management dialogs provide greater clarity.

##### Exchange visibility management

Now, you can easily manage the visibility of realized exchanges in the diagrams. The dedicated button in the smart manipulator toolbar allows you to choose whether you want to show or hide required Exchange Items.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This is an example of managing the visibility of Operational Exchange Items in an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.

##### Realized Exchange wizard improvements

Additionally, 2024x brings a more focused approach to reviewing Activity pairs connected with existing Activity Edges in the**Realized Exchange wizards,**improving overall usability and clarity.

- In the second step, our focus has shifted solely to reviewing existing Activity Pairs connected by existing Activity Edges. This means that creating new Activity pairs or editing existing ones is no longer possible. As a result, the tool will no longer trigger the automatic generation of new elements or diagrams.
- We've introduced a new step called Affected Diagrams . This step lets you see where changes will occur after realizing elements or editing existing exchanges.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of changes in the Realized Resource Exchange creation wizard.

##### Exchange Manager improvements

In this release, we've made improvements to the**Exchange Managers**. Now, you'll find a handy**Affected Diagrams**button that opens a dialog listing all the diagrams containing elements that already realize the selected exchange.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of changes in the Operational Exchange Manager.

##### Producing and Consuming Functions dialog improvements

We've made some changes in the Producing and Consuming Activities/Functions dialogs as well. They now serve as tools for reviewing Activity pairs and easily removing any unnecessary ones.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of changes in the Producing and Consuming Activities dialog.

### Library Support

New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available:

- UJTL (20230616)
- DISR ( 20230523 )

You can choose the library version when creating a new project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using libraries' space='']>]]>

### Templates and Perspectives

###### New template

- A new **EA Project Template with Process Guide**is now available in UAF. This Enterprise Architecture (EA) Guide defines a workflow for creating EA views following the Unified Architecture Framework Modeling Language (UAFML). When using this template, you will receive a new UAF project enhanced with the EA Guide for UAF. This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.

###### Renamed

- The**UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project**. Using the**EA with BPMN Project**template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- The perspective**UAF Enterprise Architect**was renamedto**EA-BPMN Architect.**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Perspective dialog' space='']>>]]>

###### Removed

- The**UAF EA Project Template with Process Guide**was removed.

### Other Improvements

- The nested ports in all relevant viewpoints are now supported.
- The options for Information Flows in the Environment Options dialog under the UAF options group are no longer available.
- The Tools option group containing the Autowire Parts action is removed from the UAF Parametric Diagram toolbar.
- The Delegate Port(s) action is now implemented in the Internal Connectivity Diagrams and is available from the diagrams toolbar and context menu of roles, connectors, and ports .
- You can now create the Vision Statement in the**Specification**window of the Vision element.
- New correctness validation rules regarding the Motivated By relationship are now available. These rules check if the Motivated By relationship is created between correct pairs.
- New correctness validation rules regarding the Impacted By relationship are now available. These rules check if the Impacted By relationship is created between correct pairs.
- A new passive validation rule COR2361 is implemented. This rule checks if an Operation Port Class Property type is correct.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h1 style="text-align: center;">UAF 1.2 Plugin</h1><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(122,134,154);">Released on: November 10, 2023</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>In the 2024x version of UAF, significant enhancements have been introduced. Behavior to Structure Synchronization has been enriched, allowing the automatic construction of structure models from defined activities and enabling one-click generation of Internal Connectivity diagrams.</p><p>Diagramming improvements include specialized Security Process Flow diagrams aligned with Operational and Resource viewpoints and new NAFv4 Structure diagrams for Logical, Service, and Physical Resource Specification Viewpoints.</p><p>Additionally, the Strategic Viewpoint now offers simplified assembly tools, such as the Strategic Motivation Table and the Impact Matrix, enhancing analysis and traceability. Exchange management has been refined for better visibility control, and various dialogs have been optimized for efficient review and management. These enhancements, along with improved library support and renamed perspectives, provide you with a more intuitive and efficient modeling experience.</p><p><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To download the latest version, see </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files" rel="nofollow">Downloading installation files</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For further information, check the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2" rel="nofollow">product documentation</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-UAFBehaviortoStructureSynchronization" rel="nofollow">UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization</a></strong></li><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-Diagramming" rel="nofollow">Diagramming</a></strong><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-NewSecurityProcessFlowdiagrams" rel="nofollow">New Security Process Flow diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-NewNAFv4Structurediagrams" rel="nofollow">New NAFv4 Structure diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-NewviewsinStrategicViewpoint" rel="nofollow">New views in Strategic Viewpoint</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-Exchangemanagementimprovements" rel="nofollow">Exchange management improvements</a></strong><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-Exchangevisibilitymanagement" rel="nofollow">Exchange visibility management</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-RealizedExchangewizardimprovements" rel="nofollow">Realized Exchange wizard improvements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-ExchangeManagerimprovements" rel="nofollow">Exchange Manager improvements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-ProducingandConsumingFunctionsdialogimprovements" rel="nofollow">Producing and Consuming Functions dialog improvements</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-LibrarySupport" rel="nofollow">Library Support</a></strong></li><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-TemplatesandPerspectives" rel="nofollow">Templates and Perspectives</a></strong></li><li><strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/2024x+Version+News#id-2024xVersionNews-OtherImprovements" rel="nofollow">Other Improvements</a></strong></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization</h1><p>In the 2024x version, the UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization has been enhanced. Now, you can automatically build the structure model according to defined Activities and generate an Internal Connectivity diagram by executing a single-click command.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating structure models by behavior models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model_news.png" /></ac:image></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Diagramming</h1></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>New Security Process Flow diagrams</h3><p>With version 2024x, the Security Process flow diagram has been specialized to align with two distinct Operational and Resource viewpoints, which led to the creation of two new diagrams: Security Process Flow (Operational) and Security Process Flow (Resource).</p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Security Process Flow diagrams.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3>New NAFv4 Structure diagrams</h3><p>New structure diagrams have been implemented in the Logical, Service, and Physical Resource Specification Viewpoints:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" /></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="S2 Service Structure" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P2 Resource Structure" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3>New views in Strategic Viewpoint</h3><p>Beginning with this version, the following new views related to the Strategic Viewpoint are introduced:</p><ul><li><strong>Strategic Motivation Table</strong>, which you can find under the UAF Strategy Diagrams category, is designed to simplify the assembly of UAF Strategic requirements, such as Enterprise Goals and Enterprise Objectives, in a tabular format. </li><li><strong>Impact Matrix</strong>, which you can find under the UAF Analysis Diagrams category, illustrates the mapping between the Strategic, Operational, Resource, Service Viewpoint Elements and Strategic Viewpoint Elements using the Impacted By relationship.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Exchange management improvements</h1><p style="text-align: left;">This version adds significant enhancements to working with exchanges, including the ability to control their visibility on diagrams. Improved exchange creation wizards and management dialogs provide greater clarity.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;">Exchange visibility management</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Now, you can easily manage the visibility of realized exchanges in the diagrams. The dedicated button in the smart manipulator toolbar allows you to choose whether you want to show or hide required Exchange Items.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_operational_exchanges.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This is an example of managing the visibility of Operational Exchange Items in an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.</h6><h3><span>Realized Exchange wizard improvements </span></h3><p><span>Additionally, 2024x brings a more focused approach to reviewing Activity pairs connected with existing Activity Edges in the </span><strong>Realized Exchange wizards,</strong><span> improving overall usability and clarity.</span></p><ul><li>In the second step, our focus has shifted solely to reviewing existing Activity Pairs connected by existing Activity Edges. This means that creating new Activity pairs or editing existing ones is no longer possible. As a result, the tool will no longer trigger the automatic generation of new elements or diagrams.</li><li>We've introduced a new step called<span> </span><strong>Affected Diagrams</strong>. This step lets you see where changes will occur after realizing elements or editing existing exchanges.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Exchange management changes.png" /></ac:image></span></span><span>An example of changes in the Realized Resource Exchange creation wizard.</span></h6><h3><span>Exchange Manager improvements</span></h3><p>In this release, we've made improvements to the<span> </span><strong>Exchange Managers</strong>. Now, you'll find a handy<span> </span><strong>Affected Diagrams</strong><span> </span>button that opens a dialog listing all the diagrams containing elements that already realize the selected exchange.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="echanges_exchange_manager.png" /></ac:image></span></span><span>An example of changes in the Operational Exchange Manager. </span></h6><h3><span>Producing and Consuming Functions dialog improvements</span></h3><p>We've made some changes in the Producing and Consuming Activities/Functions dialogs as well. They now serve as tools for reviewing Activity pairs and easily removing any unnecessary ones.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changes_producing_consuming_activities.png" /></ac:image></span>An example of changes in the Producing and Consuming Activities dialog.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Library Support</h1><p>New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available:</p><ul><li>UJTL (20230616)</li><li>DISR (<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">20230523</span>)</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can choose the library version when creating a new project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about using libraries >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Templates and Perspectives</h1></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>New template</h4><ul><li>A new<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>EA Project Template with Process Guide</strong><span> </span>is now available in UAF. This Enterprise Architecture (EA) Guide defines a workflow for creating EA views following the Unified Architecture Framework Modeling Language (UAFML). When using this template, you will receive a new UAF project enhanced with the EA Guide for UAF. This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.</span></li></ul><h4>Renamed </h4><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The<span> </span><strong>UAF Enterprise Architecture Project</strong><span> </span>was renamed to<span> </span><strong>EA with BPMN Project</strong>. Using the<span> </span><strong>EA with BPMN Project</strong><span> </span>template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The perspective<span> </span><strong style="text-align: left;">UAF Enterprise Architect</strong><span> was renamed </span>to<span> </span><strong style="text-align: left;">EA-BPMN Architect. </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Select Perspective dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the perspectives >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><h4>Removed</h4><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The<span> </span><strong>UAF EA Project Template with Process Guide</strong><span> </span>was removed.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Other Improvements</h1><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The nested ports in all relevant viewpoints are now supported.</span></li><li>The options for<span> </span><strong>Information Flows</strong><span> </span>in the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog under the<span> </span><strong>UAF<span> </span></strong>options group are no longer available.</li><li>The<span> </span><strong>Tools</strong><span> </span>option group containing the<span> </span><strong>Autowire Parts</strong><span> </span>action is removed from the UAF Parametric Diagram toolbar.</li><li>The<span> </span><strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong><span> </span>action is now implemented in the Internal Connectivity Diagrams and is available from the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">diagrams toolbar and context menu of roles, connectors, and ports</span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">You can now create the Vision Statement in the<span> </span><strong>Specification<span> </span></strong>window of the Vision element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">New correctness validation rules regarding the Motivated By relationship are now available. These rules check if the Motivated By relationship is created between correct pairs.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">New correctness validation rules regarding the Impacted By relationship are now available. These rules check if the Impacted By relationship is created between correct pairs.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">A new passive validation rule COR2361 is implemented. This rule checks if an Operation Port Class Property type is correct.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00005: A1 Meta-Data Definitions

- page_id: `191927345`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927345/A1+Meta-Data+Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:05:28.875+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a1-meta-data-definitions', 'a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831963963

831963965

831963964

**Description**

The A1 Meta-Data Definitions view specifies the categories of meta-data tag used throughout the architecture.

Architectures, particularly large ones, usually require meta-data tags to aid with searching and discovery. NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:

- Definition.
- Assumption.
- Finding.
- Recommendation.
- Purpose.
- Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.

**Implementation**

A1 can be represented using :

- A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='A1.png']
- A1 MS Word document report.
- DARS report.

831963962

## PAGE 00009: A8 Standards

- page_id: `191927353`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927353/A8+Standards
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:46:51.257+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a8-standards', 'a8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832226568

832226571

832226570

**Description**

A8 Standards table describes the current standards of the architecture and the systems elements that should conform to them. A8 Standards Forecast table provides a description of emerging standards and their potential impact on current systems and services view elements within a set of time frames within the architecture.

**Implementation**

A8 can be represented using:

- A8 Standards table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_standards_table.png']
- A8 Standards Forecast table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_forecast.png']

832226567

A8 Standards Forecast table provides a description of emerging standards and their potential impact on current systems and services view elements within a set of time frames within the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>A8 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A8 Standards table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="A8 Standards table" ac:alt="A8 Standards table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A8_standards_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="A8 Standards" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>A8 Standards Forecast table.<br /><ac:image ac:title=" A8 Standards Forecast table" ac:alt=" A8 Standards Forecast table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A8_forecast.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="A8 Standards" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f1d48e-98c2-45af-a25a-8333b806f9ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">832226567</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb114147-05b2-43ae-9c45-43453f339be3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928918 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00023: Actual Milestone Kind

- page_id: `191928918`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928918/Actual+Milestone+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:27:33.472+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Actual Measurement. Its enumeration literals are:

- InService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the configuration goes into service.
- Deployed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is a configuration deployment milestone.
- NoLongerUsed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the deployed configuration is no longer used.
- OutOfService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the in service configuration goes out of service.
- Other - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is not one of the standard Actual Milestone Kinds.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Actual Measurement. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>InService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the configuration goes into service.</li><li>Deployed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is a configuration deployment milestone.</li><li>NoLongerUsed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the deployed configuration is no longer used.</li><li>OutOfService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the in service configuration goes out of service.</li><li>Other - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is not one of the standard Actual Milestone Kinds.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><br /></p>
````

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928922 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: Actual Outcome

- page_id: `191928922`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928922/Actual+Outcome
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:28:49.897+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. The Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect".

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. The Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Instance Specification</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928108 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00029: Actual Person Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `191928108`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928108/Actual+Person+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-03-24T14:02:47.351+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['assign-actual-person', 'actual-post', 'personnel-availability']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

276394815

276394817

276394814

**On this page**

276394816

The **Actual Person Assignment Wizard** is used to assign an Actual Person for the Actual Post in specified time frame.

To open the **Actual Person****Assignment Wizard**

1. Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Actual Post and from the shortcut menu, select Add Actual Person . The Actual Person Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### **Actual Person** selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newActual Person that will be assigned for the Actual Post.

When you select (or create) an Actual Person, the relationship Fills Post is created between Actual Person and Actual Post. Start and End Dates are set to Fills Post Relationship. The bar with Actual Person is displayed in the Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Person for the Actual Post

1. In the Select Actual Post dialog, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select package where you want to create an Actual Post.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Person, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Actual Person is being used in the Actual Post.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Actual Person is being used in the Actual Post.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1602141601

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

An actual, specific post, an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America." where the Post would be president.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Instance Specification

A time-limited endeavour to create a specific set of products or services.

A temporary endeavor undertaken to create Resources or Desired Effects.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928112 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: Actual Project Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `191928112`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928112/Actual+Project+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-29T08:03:48.845+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Actual Project Assignment Wizard** is used to show Actual Projects in a specified time frame.

To open the **Actual Project****Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram (UAF).
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project or Add Sub Project .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the needed project, and from the shortcut menu, select and select Add new Sub Project .
  - In the diagram toolbar, click Add Existing. The Actual Project Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Project selection

In this step, you can select a Project in the following ways:

- Select an existing Project from the suggested list .
- Create a new Project by clicking the Creation Mode button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Project

1. In the Actual Project Assignment Wizard dialog, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Project.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Project, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Actual Project starts.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Actual Project ends.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- page_id: `191928926`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928926/Actual+Project+Milestone

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928116 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00034: Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard

- page_id: `191928116`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928116/Actual+Project+Milestone+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:11:22.097+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['actual-project-milestone-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.

To open the **Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard**

1. Open an AcV-2 (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (DoDAF 2.0), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (UAF).
2. Select a project.
3. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
4. Select one of the available milestone kinds: The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other

The wizard consists of five steps:

truenone

##### Actual Project Milestone name, kind, and date specification

In this step, you can specify the name of a new Actual Project Milestone you are creating.

You can select one of the Actual Project Milestone kinds, which are:

- In Service
- Deployed
- No Longer Used
- Out Of Service
- Other

Also, you can set the Actual Project Milestone date and time that indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information about the date and time, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Actual Project Milestone type specification

You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:

- DLOD Milestone
- DOTMLPF Milestone

You can create your own milestone type as well using the **Creation Mode** and selecting the **Create** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Version Released specification

You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Released property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Version Withdrawn specification

You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Withdrawn property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Project status specification at a particular Actual Project Milestone

After creating a new Actual Project Milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.

|  | DLOD Milestone type | DOTMLPF Milestone type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Project threads | EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTraining | DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilities |
| Status indicators | DLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues | Materiel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & Support |

You can also define your own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.

To create a project thread and status indicator

1. In the Project Thread area, click the Add button.
2. Specify the thread name.
3. In the Status Indicator area, click the Add button.
4. Specify the indicator name.
5. Click the Fill Color button.
6. In the Color dialog select the color and click OK . The project thread and status indicator are created.

To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread select the **Creation Mode,** and click the **Clone** button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.</p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open an AcV-2 (MODAF),<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Lr Lines of Development]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-2 Project Timelines" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF 2.0), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> (UAF).</li><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The wizard consists of five steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97f66a82-51e3-46dd-aea3-309376400102"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Actual Project Milestone name, kind, and date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the name of a new Actual Project Milestone you are creating.</p><p>You can select one of the Actual Project Milestone kinds, which are:</p><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul><p>Also, you can set the Actual Project Milestone date and time that indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information about the date and time, see<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Actual Project Milestone type specification</h3><p>You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:</p><ul><li>DLOD Milestone</li><li>DOTMLPF Milestone</li></ul><p>You can create your own milestone type as well using the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> and selecting the <strong>Create</strong> button.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Version Released specification</h3><p>You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Released property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_III.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Version Withdrawn specification</h3><p>You can select an existing Versioned Element to assign it as the Version Withdrawn property of the Actual Project Milestone. You can also create a new Versioned Element in this step.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_IV.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Project status specification at a particular Actual Project Milestone</h3><p>After creating a new Actual Project Milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>DLOD Milestone type</th><th>DOTMLPF Milestone type</th></tr><tr><td>Project threads</td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Status indicators</td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You can also define your own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create a project thread and status indicator</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the thread name.</li><li>In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the indicator name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Color</strong> dialog select the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The project thread and status indicator are created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e561acb7-083d-467f-9335-74eb922f2c5a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread select the <strong>Creation Mode,</strong> and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_V.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832335041

832335043

832335042

The Actual Resources (Ar) domain shows the analysis.e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations and actual relationships between them.

832335040

**Views**

## PAGE 00042: Actual Resources Connectivity

- page_id: `191928081`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928081/Actual+Resources+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:59:29.356+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832380838

832380840

832380839

**Description**

An Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain shows the communication of actual resource. It illustrates the actual resource configurations and actual relationships between them.

An Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency) shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time.

The intended usage of the actual form of the Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain includes:

- 
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Connectivity (Ar-Cn) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resources Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

832380836
