<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928122 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard

- page_id: `191928122`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928122/Actual+Resources+Deployment+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-09-18T13:00:20.508+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment', 'wizard', 'actual-resources']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This wizard is used to create the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (MODAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (NAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (UAF)

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following :
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram, and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard opens.

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show St-Rm Actual Deployment/CV-5/StV-5/NCV-5 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

The wizard consists of six steps:

truenone

##### Specifying the name and owning package

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Actual Enterprise Phase

In this step, select the existing or create a new Actual Enterprise Phase which will be represented as a key element in the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose Enterprise Phase, the Actual Enterprise Phase is created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase

1. In the Select Actual Enterprise Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Enterprise Phase, type its name.

##### Specifying start date

In this step, specify the start date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

When you select the Actual Enterprise Phase with the defined Start and End dates, those dates are selected in the calendar. Although, the Actual Enterprise Phase might not have any dates defined, or defined only the Start date. If you specified the End date for the Actual Enterprise Phase, you need to define the Start date also.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying end date

In this step, specify the end date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

If the Actual Enterprise Phase has the End date defined, this date is selected in the calendar. The End date cannot be earlier than the Start date.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Capabilities

In this step, select existing or create new Capabilities which will be represented as columns in the table.

Once Capabilities are added, the Phases relationships between Actual Enterprise Phases and Capabilities are created.

If the Actual Enterprise Phases already have Phases relationships with Capabilities, they will be displayed in the **Selected Elements** section.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Capability

1. In the Select Capabilities step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Capability.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Capability, type its name.

##### Selecting Actual Responsible Resources

In this step, select existing or create new Actual Responsible Resources which will be represented as rows of the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and added in the **Selected Elements** section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Responsible Resource

1. In the Select Actual Responsible Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create an Actual Responsible Resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. Select the needed Actual Responsible Resource.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Responsible Resource, type its name.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928129 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: Actual Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `191928129`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928129/Actual+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:13:25.213+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment-wizard', 'actual-resources-deployment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Select Actual Resources

In this step, select the Actual Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing ActualResources or create new ones.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the**Type**radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and displayed in blue in the**Selected Elements**section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Deployment date specification

In this step, you can specify the Deployment date and time for the selected Actual Resource configuration to be deployed and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The Deployment date for the Actual Resource usage can not be later than the No Longer Used date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### No longer used date specification

In this step, you can specify the No Longer Used date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be no longer used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The No longer Used date for the Actual Resourceusage can not be earlier than the Deployment date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Elements that can beActual Resources are listed in the following table.

| Domain | Actual Resources | Types of Actual Resources |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Security | Actual ResourceActual Resource | Security EnclaveResource Mitigation |
| Resources | Actual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceFielded CapabilityActual ResourceActual Resource | Natural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactCapability ConfigurationSystemResource Architecture |
| Projects | Actual Project | Project |
| Personnel | Actual ResponsibilityActual PersonActual PostActual Organization | ResponsibilityPersonPostOrganization |
| Operational | Actual Resource | Known Resource |
| Actual Resources | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource |

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


**Description**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain is represented by:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928085 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: Actual Resources Structure

- page_id: `191928085`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928085/Actual+Resources+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:55:29.756+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832612334

832612336

832612335

**Description**

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain describes the analysis, e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations as it provides a means to capture different solution architectures. The detailed analysis (trade-off, what-if etc.) is carried out using the Resource Constraints view. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations required to meet an operational need.

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is divided in two forms:

- Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. The intended usage of the typical form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) view includes:
  - Organizational analysis.
  - Definition of human roles.
  - Operational analysis.
- Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time. The intended usage of the actual form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain includes:
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Resources Structure diagram

## PAGE 00047: Actual Resources Taxonomy

- page_id: `191928088`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928088/Actual+Resources+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-01-07T13:43:14.203+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1476440482

1476440483

1476440483

**Description**

The Actual Resources Taxonomy (Ar-Tx) domain illustrates the actual organizational structure and relationships among actual organizations, actual posts, and actual persons filling those actual post, that are the key players in the architecture.

This domain describes the hierarchy of control within actual organizations, between actual posts and actual persons filling those actual posts that affect the architecture and how it is used. It is the instance version of the personnel structure which defines the types of organizations and post, etc.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Taconomy (Ar-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Description**

A phase of an actual enterprise, mission, Value Stream or Enduring Task endeavor.

Actual Strategic Phase is abstract.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Instance Specification

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928133 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `191928133`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928133/Actual+Strategic+Phase+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-09-21T13:21:37.568+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['assign-actual-person', 'actual-post', 'personnel-availability']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

276394815

INLINE

276394817

INLINE

276394816

INLINE

The **Actual Strategic Phase Wizard** is used to assign an Actual Strategic Phase.

To open the **Actual Strategic Phase****Assignment Wizard**

1. Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Add New Enterprise Mission , Add New Actual Enduring Task , Add New Whole Life Enterprise , Add New Actual Enterprise Phase , or Add New Value Stream .
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
  - In the chart, right-click a selected Actual Strategic Phase and choose Add New Sub Actual Strategic Phase or Add Existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
  - The Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### **Strategic Phase**selection

In this step, you can select a Strategic Phase in three available ways:

- Select an existing Strategic Phase as a type from the suggested list .
- Create a new Strategic Phase as a type by clicking the Creation Mode button.
- Create an instance without a type, choose UNSPECIFIED.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Select Strategic Phase dialog, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Strategic Phase.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Strategic Phase, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase starts.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase ends.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1587085761

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927176 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: Adding Actual Project Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `191927176`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927176/Adding+Actual+Project+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:05:50.153+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > **Actual Project** Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Actual Project</strong> </span><strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927177 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00056: Adding Actual Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `191927177`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927177/Adding+Actual+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:07:40.398+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagra m toolbar, click **Add New** > **Actual Sub Project**.
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project**,**choose**Add new Actual Sub Project.******
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual</span> Project</strong> dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual S</span>ub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagra<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">m toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Actual Sub Project.</strong><strong><br /></strong></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926948 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00057: Adding actual projects to NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926948`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926948/Adding+actual+projects+to+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-19T08:02:31.590+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.

To add a new actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.
2. Specify a time frame for the actual project.

To add a new actual sub project

1. Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.

To add an existing actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual sub project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click <strong>Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926914 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00058: Adding capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `191926914`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926914/Adding+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T10:52:26.455+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new capability

- On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a new capability provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ad34cb3-d429-4779-b639-b6814c5255ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928423 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: Adding custom columns in tables

- page_id: `191928423`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928423/Adding+custom+columns+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T11:07:46.102+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing tables
- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5d35fe-1fbe-4d0e-8d88-8ad29bae7b56"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding custom columns in tables" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc0393f3-60e1-4ab4-a6ef-1695f9a65e27"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927047 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00060: Adding Existing Resource Exchanges to NSV-6 table

- page_id: `191927047`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927047/Adding+Existing+Resource+Exchanges+to+NSV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T13:23:24.600+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.

To add existing Resource Exchanges

1. Click the Add Existing and select the Resource Exchange Kind:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
2. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add existing Resource Exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> and select the  Resource Exchange Kind:<ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="018e2a36-4f7f-41d9-bae8-ea1ae22a91b2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange identifier, Resource <span>Exchange </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.</li><li>You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926952 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: Adding Milestones in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926952`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926952/Adding+Milestones+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-19T10:36:26.684+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a milestone

1. Select an actual project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
5. Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926915 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00064: Adding sub capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `191926915`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926915/Adding+sub+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T10:52:41.723+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A dd existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A<strong>dd existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da049ff8-52ce-4793-997c-e889f70f4ec3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928311 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors

- page_id: `191928311`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928311/Allocate+Activities+to+State+Internal+Behaviors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T08:22:58.956+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['activities-allocation', 'states', 'internal-behavior']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

890462394

890462398

890462397

You can allocate Activities to States in all state diagrams. Activities allocation in the UAF profile works in a similar way as in SySML.

To allocate an Activity to a State

1. Select an Activity, drag and hold it over the State shape in the diagram.
2. When the tool-tip appears, release the mouse button. The context menu appears.
3. Select one of the following:
  - Entry
  - Do Activity
  - Exit

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The context menu appears.</li><li>Select one of the following:<ul><li>Entry</li><li>Do Activity</li><li>Exit</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928454 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `191928454`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928454/Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-03T15:50:48.246+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Measurements can be applied to Measurable Elements and Property Sets. 
 
To make a Measurable Element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least. For a Property Set, it is sufficient to define measurements as an owned property.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Set property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser onto the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set, and you can use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Actual Measurements can be applied to Measurable Elements and Property Sets. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To make a Measurable Element measurable</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d59253d8-7cf4-4b58-8f11-d7a60bbcb293"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a Property Set, it is sufficient to define measurements as an owned property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.</li><li>Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the <strong>Measurement Set </strong>property.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1bdd46-a069-43f5-9ae1-491dc7d1eb93"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser onto the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Applying Actual Measurements on Property Set.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set, and you can use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208651de-8398-4fae-8b88-43d30065bbd0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926954 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: Applying actual project status in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926954`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926954/Applying+actual+project+status+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-19T10:39:17.036+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To apply the actual project status to a new milestone

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the actual project status by following steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

To edit the actual project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

Specify Project Status

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To apply the actual project status to a new milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Apply Status</strong>.</li><li>Specify the actual project status by following steps of the <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the actual project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d13ec7f-596d-4186-95e6-648e27b2c7f7"><ac:rich-text-body>If two or more milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Specify Project Status</strong> and then select the milestone you want to edit the status for.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928312 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: Applying industrial symbols

- page_id: `191928312`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928312/Applying+industrial+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-13T07:52:17.785+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.

If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can

- create Performer with Image or Concept Role with Image from the toolbar (1)
- click the Image Library item on the element smart manipulator (2)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways of using Image Library

Use the **Select Image** dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.

Recently used

You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.</p><p>If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can</p><ul><li>create <strong>Performer with Image</strong> or <strong>Concept Role with Image</strong> from the toolbar (1)</li><li>click the<strong> Image Library</strong> item on the element smart manipulator (2)</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="industrial_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying industrial symbols" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Two ways of using Image Library</h6><p>Use the <strong>Select Image</strong> dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5835c62e-a8be-49d7-9f88-05f425b41c87"><ac:rich-text-body>You can easily find recently used symbols by opening the <strong>Recently used</strong> tab.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928314 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: Applying military symbols

- page_id: `191928314`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928314/Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-01-11T12:34:15.075+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.

If you will need to use the military symbols:

- When creating a new project select the needed library version.
- In the Project Options dialog, General > UAF options group, change the Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library option value from None to the needed library version.

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Symbol dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Open the Specification window of any UAF element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-1_military.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying military symbols" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9bf41ba-ceda-42d3-b604-37c4050c745e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.</p><p>If you will need to use the military symbols:</p><ul><li><span>When creating a new project </span>select the needed library version.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong> options group, change the <strong>Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</strong> option value from <strong>None</strong> to the needed library version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply military symbol for resource shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Select Symbol dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Symbol </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /></span></span><ul><li>Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Select resource and click <ac:image ac:title="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology" ac:alt="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Open the <strong>Specification window</strong> of any UAF element, select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:title="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology" ac:alt="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ul><span class="confluence-link"> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Select Symbol dialog" ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying military symbols" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.<br /></span></li></ol><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928943 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: Architectural Description

- page_id: `191928943`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928943/Architectural+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:37:10.921+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Architecture Description is a work product used to express the Architecture of some System Of Interest.

It provides executive-level summary information about the architecture description in a consistent form to allow quick reference and comparison between architecture descriptions -- It includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Architecture Description is a work product used to express the Architecture of some System Of Interest.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">It provides executive-level summary information about the architecture description in a consistent form to allow quick reference and comparison between architecture descriptions -- It includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Package</p><p><br /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract element that represents a generic architecture. Subtypes are Logical Architecture and Physical Architecture.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

## PAGE 00078: Architecture Management

- page_id: `191927371`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927371/Architecture+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-28T16:24:14.136+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Architecture Management** (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Architecture Management</strong> (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927374 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: Architecture Management Information

- page_id: `191927374`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927374/Architecture+Management+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-28T16:25:28.146+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Architecture Management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832800706

832800708

832800707

**Description**

The **Architecture Management Information**(Am-If) view contains definitions of terms used in the given architecture. It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).

**Implementation**

An Architecture Management Information****(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:

- Dictionary table . It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.
- Reports : - Dictionary (.docx). - Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx). - Dictionary Tabular (.docx).

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Architecture Management Information<strong> </strong>(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:</p><ul><li><strong>Dictionary table</strong>. It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.</li><li><strong>Reports</strong>:<br />- Dictionary (.docx).<br />- Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).<br />- Dictionary Tabular (.docx).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Dictionary" ac:alt="Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dictionary.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Architecture Management Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70a992ae-d97a-449a-bf46-820311b74ab9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">832800704</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Definition" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alias" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Same As" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927344 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `191927344`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927344/Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:22:57.262+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831912465

831912467

831912466

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

831912464

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d74279e3-81bb-4807-8202-f78c9f5a9d3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">831912465</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bd9c955-4c01-4338-9f6b-f94bad1567df"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">831912467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66df766d-1a5a-4c67-8f8a-36b9778f29de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">831912466</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="59481fea-662d-406e-9057-8c40fb8ca4d2">The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eae14fcd-2607-4c68-b2b7-b416921a84b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">831912464</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f499e560-296b-4f5a-afcb-770fc2391b79" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

**Description**

Information associated with an Architectural Description, that supplements the standard set of tags used to summarize the Architecture. It states things like what methodology was used, notation, etc.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Comment

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927059 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: Assigning resources in NSV-8 chart

- page_id: `191927059`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927059/Assigning+resources+in+NSV-8+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-19T15:30:23.373+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.

To assign a resource

1. Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select Resources . The Select Resources dialog opens.
2. Select the desired resource and click OK .

- You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.
- Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.</p><p><br /></p><p>To assign a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Resources</strong>. The <strong>Select Resources</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the desired resource and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b87682b7-f108-4928-a253-2b77f879af40"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.</li><li>Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928137 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `191928137`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928137/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-22T09:16:49.132+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004562780

1004562782

1004562781

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

truenone

##### Selecting parts

Select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a package

Select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a diagram

Specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004562777

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926492 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `191926492`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926492/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-13T09:56:59.187+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229302728

229302730

229302729

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='av_1.png']
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

229302727

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Reference' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architecture Metadata' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.</p><p>The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:</p><ul><li>In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.</li><li>When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.</li></ul><p>The AV-1 may be used to:</p><ul><li>Scope the architecture effort.</li><li>Provide context to the architecture effort.</li><li>Define the architecture effort.</li><li>Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.</li><li>Assist search within an architecture repository.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" ac:alt="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Using the following (.docx) reports:<br /><p>- AV-1 Overview and Summary Information.</p><p>- AV-1 DARS.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.</p><p>The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d928db-eff2-4732-bb69-65fec83182d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302727</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Architectural Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Architectural Reference" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Architecture Metadata" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating DARS report" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926494 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `191926494`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926494/AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-13T09:58:11.947+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229449780

229449782

229449781

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.

229449779

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
