An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).</p><p>An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.</p><p>It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" ac:alt="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fabe6b99-9e45-45b7-8985-b2e5fc71e744"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449779</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Definition" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alias" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Same As" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926773 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: Building AcV-1 matrix

- page_id: `191926773`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926773/Building+AcV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T11:50:57.293+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.

The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.

The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To build an AcV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.</p><p>The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build an AcV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926937 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00100: Building NCV-6 matrix

- page_id: `191926937`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926937/Building+NCV-6+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:55.166+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NCV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926940 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00101: Building NCV-7 matrix

- page_id: `191926940`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926940/Building+NCV-7+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:55.234+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)****depicts which Servicescontribute to the achievement of a Capabilitydirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.

To build a NCV-7 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)<strong> </strong><span>depicts which Services </span><span>contribute to the achievement of a Capability </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-7 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Services).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities). </li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926958 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00103: Building NPV-2 matrix

- page_id: `191926958`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926958/Building+NPV-2+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-10T08:37:51.307+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NPV-2 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NPV-2 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Building NPV-2 matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927000 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00104: Building NSOV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191927000`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927000/Building+NSOV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-28T13:31:05.782+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.

To build a NSOV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Services);
2. Specify Column Scope (Operational Activities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSOV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities);</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Building NSOV-3 matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Activities </span>to Services</p><hr /><p>Double-click an empty intersection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><p>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927024 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: Building NSV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `191927024`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927024/Building+NSV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T11:44:44.492+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-12 Service Provision
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a NSV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the<strong> Rows scope</strong> (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a247679b-8dbc-485c-8372-57b68d8fbd26"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927036 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00106: Building NSV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191927036`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927036/Building+NSV-3+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:55.712+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-3 System to System Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a NSV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) <span>shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927043 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00107: Building NSV-5 matrix

- page_id: `191927043`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927043/Building+NSV-5+matrix
- version_number: 2

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

The rows of the NSV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a NSV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926769 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00109: Building StV-6 matrix

- page_id: `191926769`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926769/Building+StV-6+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:54.171+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a StV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a StV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the Rebuild button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926851 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: Building SV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `191926851`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926851/Building+SV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T11:52:09.276+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-12 Service Provision
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a SV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5789cb3-91f1-4040-a57c-9fe74ea5b33d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926865 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00111: Building SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191926865`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926865/Building+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:54.682+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) shows how resources interact twith each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how resources interact twith each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926872 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00112: Building SV-5 matrix

- page_id: `191926872`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926872/Building+SV-5+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:54.809+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the <strong>Implements</strong> relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c59401e-36e3-4dd4-8e69-d6cfa036ddac"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927247 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00113: Building the S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927247`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927247/Building+the+S4+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.809+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the [implied relations](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Using+Implied+Relations) mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities****

To create S4 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.

To map Services to Operational Activities

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Using+Implied+Relations">implied relations</a> mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities<strong> </strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To create S4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map Services to Operational Activities</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927077 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00114: C1 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `191927077`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927077/C1+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:08:45.729+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c1-capability-taxonomy', 'c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

58891928

58891931

58891929

**Description**

The C1 Capability Taxonomy view covers capabilities and the hierarchical relationships between them. It specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures – i.e. one C1 may provide the definitive list of capabilities for a number of logical and resource architectures. In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.

The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.

In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. A C1 is most commonly structured as a specialization hierarchy of capabilities, with the most general at the root and most specific at the leaves. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C3 Capability Dependency' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.

The C1 view may be used to:

- Identify an existing and required capabilities.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.
- Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.

**Implementation**

The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

58891927

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Property' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Location' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.</p><p>The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.</p><p>In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.</p><p class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C3 Capability <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927081 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: C2 Enterprise Vision

- page_id: `191927081`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927081/C2+Enterprise+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-06-15T07:34:40.111+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c2-enterprise-vision', 'c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59045021

59045023

59045022

**Description**

The C2 Enterprise Vision view provides the high-level scope for the architecture and a strategic context for the capabilities the architecture contains. In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.

The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.

The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. P1 & D2are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.

The C2 view may be used to:

- Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.
- Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.
- Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).
- Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.

It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entitiesstereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

59045020

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Enterprise Phase' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Enterprise' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Phase' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Goal' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Vision' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Enduring Task' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

````html

In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.</p><p>The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.</p><p>The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>, is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">P1 &amp; D2 </span>are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.</p><p>The C2 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="C2 Enterprise Vision" ac:alt="C2 Enterprise Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C2.png"><ri:page ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cb6c64b-871d-496e-9d33-e551da922254"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">59045020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927085 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00116: C3 Capability Dependency

- page_id: `191927085`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927085/C3+Capability+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:10:12.321+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c3-capability-dependency', 'c3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59323893

59323895

59323894

**Description**

The C3 Capability Dependency view describes the dependencies between capabilities. It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.

The C3 view contrasts with [C1](http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy)which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).

The C3 view may be used to:

- Identify capability dependencies.
- Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).

**Implementation**

The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

59323892

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C3 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.</p><p>The C3 view contrasts with <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy">C1 </a>which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).</p><p>The C3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify capability dependencies.</li><li>Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:alt=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:height="552" ac:width="542"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="C3 Capability Dependency" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cdb3277-7715-4e79-8b35-6aa092923c48"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">59323892</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C3 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927088 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00117: C4 Standard Processes

- page_id: `191927088`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927088/C4+Standard+Processes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.060+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c4-standard-processes', 'c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59932335

59932337

59932336

**Description**

The C4 Standard Processes view specifies Standard Operational Activities, and optionally traces them to the capabilities they support. Some processes are standard across the whole enterprise, or even more than one enterprise. The Standard Operational Activities can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR2']).

The C4 view may be used to:

- Specify a doctrine.
- Trace capabilities to enduring tasks.
- Trace capabilities to standard operational activities.
- Audit a capability.

**Implementation**

The C4 view can be represented using a C4 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Standard Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

59932334

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']

## PAGE 00118: C5 Effects

- page_id: `191927091`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927091/C5+Effects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-23T10:20:14.825+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c5-effects', 'c5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

60050940

60050942

60050941

**Description**

The C5 Viewpoint is concerned with identifying and describing the effects of capabilities. It defines the different measurements required to define what the architecture is meant to achieve in quantitative measures.

**Implementation**

The C5 view can be represented using the C5 Effects diagram, which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a C5 Effects diagram.

60050939

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Desires' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927096 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00119: C7 Performance Parameters

- page_id: `191927096`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927096/C7+Performance+Parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-03-03T12:52:07.993+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c7-performance-parameters', 'c7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

61788572

61788575

61788573

**Description**

The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measures apply to each Capability.

The C7 view may be used to:

- Set a Capability Requirements.
- Military Estimates.
- Strategic Reviews.
- Plan an Assumptions.

**Implementation**

The C7 view can be represented using:

- C7 Actual Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- C7 Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

61788571

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927099 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00120: C8 Planning Assumptions

- page_id: `191927099`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927099/C8+Planning+Assumptions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:52:06.629+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148777069

148777089

148777079

**Description**

The C8 Planning Assumptions Viewpoint is concerned with the identification and description of assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.

**Implementation**

The **C8 Planning Assumptions**view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . The table identifies and describes assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

148777058

**Related pages**

1993049291

1993049281

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Capability. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

1993049270

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c379c43b-bae9-42a8-a277-6b070617fd09"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1993049270</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C8 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C8 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00122: Capability

- page_id: `191928949`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An enterprise's ability to Achieve a desired effect realized through a combination of ways and means (e.g., CapabilityConfigurations) along with specified measures.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Class

- Other
- Personnel
- Resource
- Security
- Service
- Strategic

Enumeration

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928141 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: Capability Provision Creation Wizard

- page_id: `191928141`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928141/Capability+Provision+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-06-04T09:32:00.814+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['capability-provision-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Provision Creation Wizard** is used to define a Capability Provision by one or more Resources at a specific period of time.

To open the **Capability Provision Creation Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (DoDAF), StV-3 (MODAF), NCV-3 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (NAF 4.0), or Strategic Phasing diagram (UAF).
2. Select a Capability.
3. Do one of the following: The Capability Provision Creation Wizard opens.
  - On the <viewpoint name> diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resources selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newResources.

For more information on selecting elements, see **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**, Step #2.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability service date specification

In this step, you can set the date and time, when the Resource should begin provisioning the Capability. The In Service Milestone is created.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Out of service date specification (Optional)

In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The Out Of Service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the Capability exhibition bar on the chart will have a triangle ending.

For more information on setting date ant time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='NCV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926497 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: Capability viewpoint

- page_id: `191926497`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926497/Capability+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:20:49.480+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['capability-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229515785

229515787

229515786

“The Capability Viewpoint and the DoDAF-described Models within the viewpoint are introduced into DoDAF V2.0 to address the concerns of Capability Portfolio Managers. In particular, the Capability Models describe capability taxonomy and capability evolution.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

“The DoD increasingly employs incremental acquisition to help manage the risks of complex procurements. Consequently, there is a need to provide visualizations of the evolving capabilities so that Portfolio Managers can synchronize the introduction of capability increments across a portfolio of projects. The Capability Models included within DoDAF are based on the program and capability information used by Portfolio Managers to capture the increasingly complex relationships between interdependent projects and capabilities.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo:

- How to model and relate capabilities to each other, watch demo**[*UPDM: Strategic**.**Part 1*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM).
- How to relate capabilities to the elements from other viewpoints and analyze capability provision and deployment gaps,watch demo **[*UPDM: Strategic**.**Part 2*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ) *.*

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

229515784

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928352 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: Case Study

- page_id: `191928352`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928352/Case+Study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-13T10:16:56.439+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Displaying information indirectly related to element through Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let’s say, we need to show Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements that are produced and consumed by the performer Search Node. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the Search Node element.

We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.

First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the Search Node.

Let’s create the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the performer *Search Node*. Open the *Search Node* **Specification** window and in the **Traceability** property group click the **Create** button. In the opened **Criterion Editor** dialog, select the **Meta Chain** tab. Then specify the derived property name and the expression for the meta chain as it is shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification of derived property name and expression

In the following figure, you can see that a new subgroup named **Other** is created in the performer **Specification**window. Our created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is placed under this subgroup. The **Produces Exchange Element** derived property has *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements as its values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of newly created derived property

Next, we need to display values of the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the *Search Node* performer in the OV-2 diagram.

A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all performers, not only to*Search Node*.

We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the *Search Node*. Let’s draw a Note and connect it to the Search Node performer. Then click the Edit Compartment as it is shown in the following figure. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab. From the properties list, select to display the **Produces Exchange Element** property. After clicking the **OK** button, *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements are displayed in the Note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Information indirectly related to Search Node is displayed in Note

Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the *Search Node*, as well as to other performers. For that purpose, we select Performer as an **Element Type** and add existing performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is also available to add as a new column through the **Show Columns** menu (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Produces Exchange Element derived property displayed as column

Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same **Criterion Editor** and doing the same actions as described previously. In the following figure is the example of the **Consumes Exchange Element** derived property expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Consumes Exchange Element derived property expression

When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Consumes Exchange Element derived property added as new column

#### PANEL: Related procedure

Related procedure

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying information indirectly related to element through Derived Properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Let’s say, we need to show Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements that are produced and consumed by the performer Search Node. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the Search Node element.</p><p>We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.</p><p>First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the Search Node.</p><p>Let’s create the <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> derived property for the performer <em>Search Node</em>. Open the <em>Search Node</em> <strong>Specification</strong> window and in the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened <strong>Criterion Editor</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Meta Chain</strong> tab. Then specify the derived property name and the expression for the meta chain as it is shown in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specification of derived property name and expression</h6><p>In the following figure, you can see that a new subgroup named <strong>Other</strong> is created in the performer <strong>Specification </strong>window. Our created <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> derived property is placed under this subgroup. The <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> derived property has <em>Warning Order</em> and <em>Medical Condition</em> exchange elements as its values.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of newly created derived property</h6><p>Next, we need to display values of the <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> derived property for the <em>Search Node</em> performer in the OV-2 diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073cc4b6-5bb5-4f7b-85af-bd43a30b1661"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all performers, not only to<em> Search Node</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the <em>Search Node</em>. Let’s draw a Note and connect it to the Search Node performer. Then click the Edit Compartment as it is shown in the following figure. In the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab. From the properties list, select to display the <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> property. After clicking the <strong>OK</strong> button, <em>Warning Order</em> and <em>Medical Condition</em> exchange elements are displayed in the Note.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_III.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Information indirectly related to Search Node is displayed in Note</h6><p>Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the <em>Search Node</em>, as well as to other performers. For that purpose, we select Performer as an <strong>Element Type</strong> and add existing performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created <strong>Produces Exchange Element</strong> derived property is also available to add as a new column through the <strong>Show Columns</strong> menu (see the following figure).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_IV.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Produces Exchange Element derived property displayed as column</h6><p>Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same <strong>Criterion Editor</strong> and doing the same actions as described previously. In the following figure is the example of the <strong>Consumes Exchange Element</strong> derived property expression.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_V.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Consumes Exchange Element derived property expression</h6><p>When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column (see the following figure).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="case_study_VI.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Study" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Consumes Exchange Element derived property added as new column</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56056506-16fd-4b38-a100-47b4499cf79e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedure</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying information indirectly related to element through Derived Properties" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge.

The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind:

- Business
- Enterprise
- Mission
- Other
- Strategic

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Enumeration

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929255 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: Changed Property Types

- page_id: `191929255`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929255/Changed+Property+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T11:27:46.836+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for the Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created with Environment set to this property. If the value was Condition, it is removed and added to ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h4>Changes for Condition and Environment</h4><p>In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property. </p><p>The same is valid for the Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. I<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">f the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created with Environment set to this property. If the value was Condition, it is removed and added to ToDo property.</span></p><p><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="concition_to_instance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Changed Property Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h4>Changes for Doctrine</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929234 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: Changed Property Types in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `191929234`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929234/Changed+Property+Types+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T13:18:57.456+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for the Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created with Environment set to this property. If the value was Condition, it is removed and added to ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.2 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h4>Changes for Condition and Environment</h4><p>In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property. </p><p>The same is valid for the Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. I<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">f the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created with Environment set to this property. If the value was Condition, it is removed and added to ToDo property.</span></p><p><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="concition_to_instance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Changed Property Types in UAF 1.2" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h4>Changes for Doctrine</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.2 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929222 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00134: Changes for Security Control

- page_id: `191929222`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929222/Changes+for+Security+Control
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:05.943+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1 > Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The new approach for modeling the Security Control (and its specialization) is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']. The profile changes are described in the following table:

| Before migration | After migration | Comment |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | Type | Name | Type |  |
| Security Control | Activity | Security Control | Class | Aditionally, Security Process is created to overtake the information, that cannot be stored in Security Control [Class]. |
| Security Control Family | Comment | Security Control Family | Class | The Criterion from SecurityControlFamily [comment] is moved to the Text of the SecurityControlFamily [class, requirement].Information about the Annotated Element is moved to ToDo. |
| Enhanced Security Control | Activity | Enhanced Security Control | Class | Aditionally, Security Process is created to overtake the information, that cannot be stored in Security Control [Class]. |
| Security Control Action | Call Behavior Action | Security Process Action | Call Behavior Action |  |
| - | - | Security Process | Activity | Security Process is a new element dedicated for modeling security behaviors. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The new approach for modeling the Security Control (and its specialization) is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The profile changes are described in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 64.1848%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.1356%;" /><col style="width: 13.8983%;" /><col style="width: 18.4746%;" /><col style="width: 12.9661%;" /><col style="width: 36.5254%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Before migration</th><th style="text-align:
