<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926774 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00326: Creating AcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926774`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926774/Creating+AcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:04:55.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.

To create an AcV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (OV-4) or Actual Posts (OV-4) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.

You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.

You can also use an AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an AcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (OV-4) or Actual Posts (OV-4) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="604b1f2d-c20b-41c3-a733-45483cde06c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9444da6-5f6c-432d-899d-0451dc051699"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use an AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928437 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00327: Creating and displaying derived properties

- page_id: `191928437`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928437/Creating+and+displaying+derived+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-23T07:30:45.892+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['create-derived-properties', 'edit-derived-properties', 'delete-derived-properties', 'display-derived-properties', 'display-information-indirectly-related-to-element-through-derived-properties']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to create derived properties to display the information indirectly related to an element. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying derived properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying derived properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying derived properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] derived properties in the:

- Element Specification window
- Table

You can manage derived properties using the **Expression** dialog.

create_newTo create a new derived property

1. Open the Expression dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, select the Traceability property group and click the Create button.
  - In the table toolbar, click Show Columns and select New Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property.
3. Click **OK**.

If you want to create a derived property with a result other than an element, you must manually change the derived property type.

editTo edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element Specification window, select the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, right-click the derived property column heading and select Edit Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog make changes and click OK .

removeTo remove a derived property

1. Do one of the following:

- In the selected element Specification window, select the Traceability property group, choose the derived property you want to remove, and click the Delete button.
- In the active table, right-click the derived property column heading and select Remove Derived Property .

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying derived properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and displaying derived properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (as a new column)

display_on_noteTo display derived properties on a Note shape

1. Create a Note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Note and click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator. The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
  - Right-click the Note shape and select Edit Compartment . The Compartment Edit dialog opens. Choose the Element Properties tab.
3. In the**Hidden** list, move the properties you want to display on a Note shape to the **Selected** list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the Note.

display_in_tableTo display derived properties in a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Show Columns button and select Select Columns .
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select the properties you want to display.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

**Case Study**

Let’s say, we need to show *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements produced and consumed by the performer *Search Node*. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the *Search Node* element.

We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.

First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the*Search Node*.

Let’s create the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the Operational Performer *Search Node*. Open the *Search Node* Specification window, and in the **Traceability** property group, click the **Create** button. In the **Expression** dialog, select the **Metachain Navigation**property group. Then specify the derived property name and the meta chain expression as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following figure shows that a new subgroup named **Other** is created in the Operational Performer Specification window. Our created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is placed under this subgroup. The **Produces Exchange Element** derived property has Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements as its values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, we need to display values of the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the Operational Performer *Search Node* in the OV-2 diagram.

A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all Operational Performers, not only to *Search Node*.

We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the Search Node. Let’s create a Note and connect it to the Operational Performer*Search Node*. Then click **Edit Element Properties**. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab. From the properties list, select to display the Produces Exchange Element property. After clicking the **OK** button, Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements are displayed in the Note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the *Search Node* as well as to other Operational Performers. For that purpose, we select Operational Performer as an**Element Type** and add existing Operational Performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property can be added as a new column through the **Show Columns** menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same **Expression** dialog and doing the same actions as described previously. The following figure is an example of the **Consumes Exchange Element** derived property expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927079 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00328: Creating C1 diagram

- page_id: `191927079`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927079/Creating+C1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:09:08.008+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C1 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927201 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00329: Creating C1-S1 matrix

- page_id: `191927201`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927201/Creating+C1-S1+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.693+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1-s1-matrix', 'build-c1-s1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix****depicts which Services contribute to the achievement of a Capability directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.****

To create C1-S1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix<strong> </strong>depicts which Services contribute to the achievement of a Capability directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.<strong> </strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To create C1-S1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927083 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00330: Creating C2 diagram

- page_id: `191927083`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927083/Creating+C2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:09:55.970+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C2 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['create-c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C2 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. Specify the types of parts. The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.

Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To complete fully the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Create Element" ac:alt="Create Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator.</li><li><p>Specify the types of parts.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7c75b24-976a-43ed-bce0-dacb135d54e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To complete fully the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927087 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00331: Creating C3 diagram

- page_id: `191927087`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927087/Creating+C3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-01-21T12:33:23.624+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C3 Capability Dependency
- labels: ['create-c3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C3 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependency relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>(recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependency relationships.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927090 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00332: Creating C4 matrix

- page_id: `191927090`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927090/Creating+C4+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.098+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C4 Standard Processes
- labels: ['create-c4', 'build-c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Standard Processes (C4)****describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a C4 matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Standard Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Standard Processes (C4)<strong> </strong><span>describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p>To create a C4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927094 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00333: Creating C5 diagram

- page_id: `191927094`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927094/Creating+C5+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-23T10:18:42.974+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C5 Effects
- labels: ['create-c5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C5 diagram

1. Create a Desirer (e.g., Capability).
2. Create an Actual State (e.g., Actual Effect) and set a type (e.g., Effect).
3. Connect a Desirer with the Actual State using the Desires relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of C5 Effects diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C5 diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Desirer (e.g., Capability).</li><li>Create an Actual State (e.g., Actual Effect) and set a type (e.g., Effect).</li><li>Connect a Desirer with the Actual State using the Desires relationship.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="c5_effects_diagram_sample.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C5 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of C5 Effects diagram.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927100 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00334: Creating C8 diagram

- page_id: `191927100`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927100/Creating+C8+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.137+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C8 Planning Assumptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1709625638

1709625659

1709625637

**On this page**

1709625649

##### Creating a diagram

To create a C8 diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the C8 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > C8 Planning Assumptions . [ATTACHMENT filename='c8_creating.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for C8 Planning Assumptions and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='c8_new_capability.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the C8 package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating and applying Strategic Constraints

You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.

To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed Capability and open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Strategic > Strategic Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.

To apply Strategic Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability.
- In the needed Capability Specification window, go to the Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

1487199865

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927105 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00335: Creating C8 table

- page_id: `191927105`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927105/Creating+C8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-08T12:04:49.156+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C8 Planning Assumptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487265801

1487265822

1487265812

The**Planning Assumptions (C8)**table identifies and describes assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.

To add a new Strategic Constraint to the C8 table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the**Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint**dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Strategic Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the C8 Planning Assumptions table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

1487265800

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 00336: Creating comments for UAF elements

- page_id: `191928330`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928330/Creating+comments+for+UAF+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.674+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Specification

Documentation/Comment

Additionally

.

Create Element

###### Creating a Definition for any UAF element

To create a Definition

1. Select an element and open its Specification window.
2. Go to the Documentation/Comment property group.
3. Click the Add button and choose Definition.
4. Add required specifics and click Close .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating Vision Statement for Enterprise Vision

To create a Vision Statement

1. Select an Enterprise Vision element and open its Specification window.
2. Go to the Documentation/Comment property group.
3. Click the Add button and choose Vision Statement.
4. Add required specifics and click Close .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927110 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00337: Creating Cr diagram

- page_id: `191927110`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927110/Creating+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:13:03.806+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['create-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Cr diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** opens.

To create a Cr diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap** check box.

To create an Cr diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create Cr diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p>To create a Cr diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e741dd50-b862-463a-b157-79fa209e36fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Cr diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12453914-2d6d-4cf8-906c-7319424ff5ab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926501 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00338: Creating CV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926501`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926501/Creating+CV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-13T10:03:03.599+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-1 Vision
- labels: ['create-cv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-1 should be the first diagram for DoDAF architectures.

To create a CV-1 diagram

1. Create a Capability andActualEnterprise Phase.
2. Connect the Capability and the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Exhibits relationship.

An additional Actual Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Enterprise Goals and the Enterprise Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Actual Enterprise Phase.

In order to complete the CV-1, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be modelled to create the Capabilities that exhibits ActualEnterprise Phase.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-1 should be the first diagram for DoDAF architectures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create a Capability and<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Enterprise Phase</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connect the Capability and the </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Enterprise Phase using the Exhibits relationship.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">An additional Actual Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Enterprise Goals and the Enterprise Vi</span>sion by using the goal and vision properties of the Actual Enterprise Phase.</p><p>In order to complete the CV-1, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modelled to create the Capabilities that exhibits <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span>Enterprise Phase.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926504 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00339: Creating CV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926504`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926504/Creating+CV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-13T10:05:08.484+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-cv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-2 should be modeled just before the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] is completed.

To create a CV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational Performers and Resources usingExhibits relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the *environmentConditions* property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-2 should be modeled just before the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational Performers and Resources using<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Exhibits</span> relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the <em>environmentConditions</em> property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926507 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00340: Creating CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `191926507`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926507/Creating+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T10:53:32.165+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['create-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

188667993

188668004

188668003

Before creating CV-3 diagram, the CV-1 diagram with all necessary elements should be already created in the model. For this, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. Otherwise, you can create those elements directly in the blank CV-3 chart. The following elements required for a smooth CV-3 diagram creation:

- Capability.
- Capability Provision.
- Incremental Milestone.
- End of Service Milestone.

The following procedures describe how to:

- Create CV-3 diagram by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Create an CV-3 diagram when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] is disabled.
- Fill in a blank CV-3 diagram.

##### Creating CV-3 diagram by using the CV-3 Creation Wizard

When you choose to create a new CV-3 diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** opens automatically.

To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create CV-3** check box.

##### Creating an CV-3 diagram when CV-3 Creation Wizard is disabled

To create an CV-3 diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** is disabled

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank CV-3 chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Filling in a blank CV-3 diagram

To fill in a blank CV-3 diagram

1. Add a Capability in the following ways: - To create new Capability, in the diagram toolbar, click the Add New button and select Capability. - To add already existing Capability from the model, in the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button and select Capability. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-12-2 16:58:42.png']
2. Type the name of added Capability.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

188667991

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF </strong>options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Filling in a blank CV-3 diagram</h3><p>To fill in a blank CV-3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Add a Capability in the following ways:<br />- To create new Capability, in the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Capability.<br />- To add already existing Capability from the model, in the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Capability.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-12-2 16:58:42.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-3 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Type the name of added Capability.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="081d0f25-554f-4bd4-980d-1ab6ee88982a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ab3110-6166-4c85-848c-dc9cd5491c51"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">188667991</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926512 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00341: Creating CV-4 diagram

- page_id: `191926512`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926512/Creating+CV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-13T10:09:30.912+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['create-cv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-4 is created just after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] is completed.

To create a CV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-4 is created just after the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926514 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00342: Creating CV-5 table

- page_id: `191926514`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926514/Creating+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-19T12:48:52.566+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

228955995

228956009

924237466

**On this page**

228955998

##### Creating CV-5 table

The context of the CV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the CV-5 table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 table

To create an empty CV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a CV-5 table

To remove the deployment of theActual Resources from a CV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

228955990

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="253d647f-78f8-46a2-8506-edf4d9cae327"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955995</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0463f24b-1ec3-4776-9e36-435f67db21a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228956009</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f493355f-de7f-4970-99c2-8b6bd73db50b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924237466</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bf22f3c-9dbf-4c51-879f-068de9457660" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af5bbcd-15a2-4587-a065-9aa2c4c4ded3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955998</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating CV-5 table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the CV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_relation_to_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The content of the CV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5124318-7a44-4b50-acc7-4bc4bfa6ad14"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.</p><p>There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p>Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a CV-5 table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a CV-5</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt;<strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>An<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>, select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Actual Resources from a CV-5 table</h3><p>To remove <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the deployment of the </span>Actual Resources from a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an Actual Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the CV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb6e248f-608d-45d2-8699-ff9b314e18f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955990</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926526 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00343: Creating CV-6 matrix

- page_id: `191926526`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926526/Creating+CV-6+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:52.229+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-6-matrix', 'relate-operational-activity-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-6-matrix', 'build-cv-6-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Operational Activities Mapping (CV-6) describes the mapping between the capabilities and the operational activities that those capabilities support directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a CV-6 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using theMaps ToCapability relationship.

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Operational Activities Mapping (CV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the capabilities and the operational activities that those capabilities support directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Maps To </span>Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map an Operational Activity to a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926530 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00344: Creating CV-7 matrix

- page_id: `191926530`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926530/Creating+CV-7+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:52.353+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-7-matrix', 'relate-service-access-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-7-matrix', 'build-cv-7-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping (CV-7) depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enabledirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].The rows of this matrix areServicesand the columns are Capabilities.

To create a CV-7 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope ( Services ).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibitsrelationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping (CV-7) <span>depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enable </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>The rows of this matrix are<span> Services </span>and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-7 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Services</span>).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Services exhibit</span> Capabilities using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927343 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00345: Creating D2 diagram

- page_id: `191927343`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927343/Creating+D2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T09:13:01.889+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Deployed Resources viewpoint > D2 Deployed Resources
- labels: ['create-d2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

To create an D2 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.
- the Persons and Actual Persons in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2012531-7254-4590-a8e5-84f712c78a1a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an D2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3260655-28c4-4062-a3ab-b66935884793"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Persons and Actual Persons in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928333 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00346: Creating Desires

- page_id: `191928333`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928333/Creating+Desires
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-10-24T07:32:48.171+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['desired-effect']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Desires relationship relates the Desirer (Capability, Resource Performer, Operational Agent, or Capability Role) to an actual state (e.g., Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Resource).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Desires relationship relates the Desirer (Capability, Resource Performer, Operational Agent, or Capability Role) to an actual state (e.g., Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Resource).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="desired_effect.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Desires" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926589 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00347: Creating DIV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926589`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926589/Creating+DIV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:39:33.416+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint > DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-1-conceptual-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a DIV-1 diagram

1. Create Information Element.
2. Connect Information Elements using Generalizations.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a DIV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Information</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Element.</span></li><li>Connect Information <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Elements</span> using Generalizations.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926592 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00348: Creating DIV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926592`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926592/Creating+DIV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:42:17.659+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint > DIV-2 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-2-logical-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a DIV-2 diagram

1. Create Information Elements.
2. Create Association relationships between Information Elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a DIV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create<span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Information </span></span>Elements.</li><li>Create Association relationships between<span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Information </span></span>Elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926595 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00349: Creating DIV-3 diagram

- page_id: `191926595`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926595/Creating+DIV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-13T06:28:11.295+02:00`

- labels: ['create-div-3-physical-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a DIV-3 diagram

1. Create Data Elements .
2. Draw Association relationships among Data Elements .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a DIV-3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Data Elements</span>.</li><li>Draw Association relationships among <span>Data Elements</span>.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928335 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00350: Creating Entity Relationship Diagram

- page_id: `191928335`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928335/Creating+Entity+Relationship+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T08:06:04.349+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Entity Relationship diagram only shows entities as boxes (without structure) and relationships between them. It is useful for high-level, abstract domain modeling - provide a structure for business data, or define business terminology.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Entity Relationship diagram only shows entities as boxes (without structure) and relationships between them. It is useful for high-level, abstract domain modeling - provide a structure for business data, or define business terminology.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928365 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00351: Creating Impact Matrix

- page_id: `191928365`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928365/Creating+Impact+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-06T07:00:17.784+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Impact Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Impact Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Strategic Element, Operational Element, Resource Element, and Service Element).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Strategic Element).
3. Click the Refresh button.

The Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements mapps to Strategic Elements using the ImpactedBy relationship.

ImpactedBy relationship is available between the following element pairs:

- Actual Outcome and Enterprise Goal
- Capability and Opportunity
- Actual Strategic Phase and Challenge
- Operational Architecture/Resource Architecture/Service Architecture and Challenge
- Operational Activity and Challenge

To map the elements

- Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

To change the matrix axes

- In the matric toolbar, click Change Axes .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Impact Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Strategic Element, Operational Element, Resource Element, and Service Element).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Strategic Element).</li><li>Click the Refresh button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements mapps to Strategic Elements using the ImpactedBy relationship.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="113ee3cb-384b-4589-abc4-43272d65dfbd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>ImpactedBy relationship is available between the following element pairs:</p><ul><li>Actual Outcome and Enterprise Goal</li><li>Capability and Opportunity</li><li>Actual Strategic Phase and Challenge</li><li>Operational Architecture/Resource Architecture/Service Architecture and Challenge</li><li>Operational Activity and Challenge</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To map the elements</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the matrix axes</p><hr /><ul><li>In the matric toolbar, click<span> </span><strong>Change Axes</strong>.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928368 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00352: Creating Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `191928368`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928368/Creating+Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.898+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Implementation Matrix
- labels: ['create-implementation-matrix', 'build-implementation-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1370480142

1370480144

1370480143

The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elementsdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

To create an Implementation Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope . The available row scopes:
  - Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.
  - Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.
  - Service Elements: Service Interface.
  - Summary & Overview Elements: Architecture.
2. Specify the Columns Scope . The available column scoper:
  - Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.
  - Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.
  - Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.
  - Resource Elements: Resource Connector.
3. Click the Refresh button.

The Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements map to Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elements using the Implements relation.

You can swap the rows and columns by changing the axes in the matrix.

To change the matrix axes

- In the matric toolbar, click Change Axes .

To map the elements

1. Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

The Implements relationship is available between the following element pairs:

- Architecture and Actual Strategic Phase.
- Function and Operational Activity or Service Function.
- Operational Activity and Actual Strategic Phase.
- Operational Information and Strategic Information.
- Resource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource Connector.
- Resource Exchange and Operational Exchange
- Resource Information and Operational Information.
- Resource Interface and Operational Interface or Service Interface.
- Resource Performer and Operational Agent.
- Resource Role and Operational Role.
- Resource Service and Service.
- Service Interface and Operational Interface.

You can choose which element pairs to show or hide in the Implementation matrix.

To show/hide element pairs in the matrix

1. In the matrix toolbar, click Show Element Pairs .
2. Click to select or clear the selection of the element pairs.

1370480141

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']
