<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927118 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

- page_id: `191927118`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927118/Creating+L1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T06:43:08.060+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-high-level-operational-concept-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.

To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.<br /></span></p><p>To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00354: Creating L1 Node Types diagram

To create an L1 Node Types diagram

1. Create Business Architectures or Nodes.
2. Connect Nodes using Node Associations.


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927123 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00355: Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram

- page_id: `191927123`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927123/Creating+L2+Logical+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.274+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an L2 Logical Scenario diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2 package, and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2 Logical Scenario**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2 Logical Scenario,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the L2 package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='L2_drag_operational_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an L2 Logical Scenario diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>In the Containment Tree, select the L2 package, and d</span>o one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> L2 Logical Scenario</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="187" ac:width="307"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>L2 Logical Scenario, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span>. An example is described using the Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="199" ac:width="477"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_operational_performer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the L2 package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_composition_pallete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="295" ac:width="847"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="316" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.</p><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="126" ac:width="501"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="279" ac:width="502"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d13e10d-1e94-4838-bbd0-5cc2c6d0a234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the <strong>Operational Association</strong> also.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="887" ac:width="727"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd4c464-ed89-4754-b3b4-2f0c0451e567"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="773" ac:width="747"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927141 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00356: Creating L2i diagram

- page_id: `191927141`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927141/Creating+L2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T08:51:48.971+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Internal Scenario
- labels: ['create-l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L2i diagram

1. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.
2. Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L2i diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74e40a9f-bcea-41a4-8f08-0d730ceaa168">Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927146 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: Creating L3 Node Interactions table

- page_id: `191927146`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927146/Creating+L3+Node+Interactions+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T09:19:53.160+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L3 Node Interactions
- labels: ['create-l3-node-interactions-table', 'add-operational-exchange-in-l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent , Receiving Operational Agent , and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an L1 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L3 Node Interactions table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L3 Node Interactions table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, Receiving Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">L1</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927150 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00358: Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `191927150`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927150/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T12:20:48.124+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram should be created first.

To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ff2594-a9e8-4506-b6f9-b31d12bef112"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Logical Activities Taxonomy</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927151 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00359: Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927151`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927151/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T12:20:34.150+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or reuse Nodes from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or reuse Nodes from <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">view </span>(recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927319 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00360: Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `191927319`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927319/Creating+L4-P4+Systems+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.295+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'build-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) addresses the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.

To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) <span>addresses the direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927160 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00361: Creating L7 diagram

- page_id: `191927160`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927160/Creating+L7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T06:46:40.805+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L7 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L7 diagram

1. Create Information Elements or Entity Items.
2. Add Properties.
3. Create Association.

Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Information Elements or Entity Items.</li><li>Add Properties.</li><li>Create Association.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927163 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00362: Creating L8 table

- page_id: `191927163`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927163/Creating+L8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-08T13:46:07.443+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['create-l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991427608

1991427628

1991427618

To create an L8 table, do either

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Information Element, and Operational Exchange). Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

1991427607

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927170 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00363: Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart

- page_id: `191927170`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927170/Creating+Lr+Lines+of+Development.+Lr+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:04:30.933+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-lines-of-development', 'create-lr-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**opens.

To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Follow the steps of the `****` [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the******[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development** check box.

To create an Lr diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927174 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00364: Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

- page_id: `191927174`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927174/Creating+Lr+Programme+Portfolio+Relationships+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:05:28.376+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-portfolio-relationships']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UAF12P2024xR2']before creating the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.

To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole) . Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) or Actual Posts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.

You can:

- Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.
- Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3513b26-7930-47fd-873b-7bb6a849f085"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating <span>the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">(part)</span> to another<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (whole)</span>. Alternatively you can specify the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> whole and part </span>properties. <span> </span></li><li><p>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or Actual Posts (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640a34e1-3325-41e0-8bbc-4d501ce74f88"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b87a6e49-5998-4084-9df0-12dd640e4b24">Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Responsibility Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927171 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00365: Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

- page_id: `191927171`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927171/Creating+Lr+Programme+to+Capability+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-10T08:39:34.633+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-to-capability-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Lr Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Lr Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927173 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00366: Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix

- page_id: `191927173`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927173/Creating+Lr+Responsibility+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T09:26:51.316+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UAF12P2024xR2']D2 diagrambefore creating the Lr Responsibility Matrix.

The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.

The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To create the Lr Responsibilitymatrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b20a7230-c44c-4ae1-8fd6-2079374a1070"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">D2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility Matrix</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility </span>matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926854 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00367: Creating MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926854`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926854/Creating+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T10:11:30.834+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926859 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00368: Creating MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926859`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926859/Creating+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-03T12:22:52.329+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926907 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00369: Creating NCV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926907`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926907/Creating+NCV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-18T11:52:13.882+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-1 Capability Vision
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.

To create a NCV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li> Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926910 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00370: Creating NCV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926910`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926910/Creating+NCV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-18T12:40:58.564+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.

To create a NCV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property *environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property <em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926913 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00371: Creating NCV-3 diagram

- page_id: `191926913`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926913/Creating+NCV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-18T13:00:04.917+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create NCV-3 diagram, **NCV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a NCV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . NCV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click **Finish** when you are done. If you do not wish to use the NCV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NCV-3”.Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the**Environment Options** dialog go to **UPDM** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a NCV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UPDM</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-3 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open window, find and select <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> diagram.</li><li>In the <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c0fb0c0-8239-4124-87b4-555bc057a73a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926921 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00372: Creating NCV-4 diagram

- page_id: `191926921`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926921/Creating+NCV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-18T13:27:29.688+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-4 is created just after the NCV-2 is completed.

To create a NCV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from NCV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-4 is created just after the NCV-2 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from NCV-2 (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926928 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00373: Creating NCV-5 table

- page_id: `191926928`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926928/Creating+NCV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-19T12:49:25.465+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

924292202

924292214

924292200

**On this page**

924292204

##### Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table

The context of the NCV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the NCV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the NCV-5 table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 table

To create an empty NCV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NATO Capability Viewpoint package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

924353687
