<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926550 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00406: Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

- page_id: `191926550`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926550/Creating+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T07:20:56.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic
- labels: ['create-ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926553 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: Creating OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926553`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926553/Creating+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T07:24:00.760+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="844e8454-fb00-4760-b817-89cb9bc6b387"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Performers.</li><li>Connect them using the Associations relationship.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</span></p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p>Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926557 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00408: Creating OV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `191926557`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926557/Creating+OV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T08:59:03.045+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.

To create an OV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab520339-ec3a-4ea9-ac87-ad3df225ba23"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.</p><p>To create an OV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Roles.</li><li>Associate Operational Roles using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926563 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: Creating OV-3 role-based table

- page_id: `191926563`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926563/Creating+OV-3+role-based+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T09:17:12.789+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table

1. In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agents</span>). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f29e07-4f97-4eae-9177-b78a40adfed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926562 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00410: Creating OV-3 table

- page_id: `191926562`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926562/Creating+OV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T09:16:35.302+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-operational-exchange-in-ov-3', 'create-ov-3-table', 'create-operational-exchange-in-ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']OV-2 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.</p><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read only.</span></li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926567 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00411: Creating OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `191926567`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926567/Creating+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T09:05:53.755+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['create-ov-4-organizational-relationship-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.

To create an OV-4 Typical diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an OV-4 Actual diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship . [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4_actual.png']

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.
- the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p><p>To create an OV-4 Typical diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Organizations, </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an OV-4 Actual diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Persons</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Relate Actual Organizational Resources with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Organization Relationship</span></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-4 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926799 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00412: Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `191926799`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926799/Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:28:09.225+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926800 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00413: Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `191926800`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926800/Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:28:26.655+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````
